derivation: # Hand-calculated derivation — Reg Z Appendix J identity case
Setup: a level-payment fully-amortizing loan with ZERO finance charges has
an APR equal to its nominal annual rate exactly. This is an algebraic
identity: the Reg Z Appendix J unit-period equation
collapses to the standard PMT equation when finance_charges = 0, advances
is a single t=0 advance of the full principal, and payments is a level
annuity. The unique i that balances the equation is therefore the same r
that defines the PMT: i = r = annual_rate / 12.
Loan: P = $100,000.00, annual rate = 12.000%, term = 360 months.
Step 2: U-equation balances exactly at i = 0.01 (1% monthly).
Engine assertion: exact Decimal equality on estimated_apr.
